description: Legal record (#77) in a court register/pinqas from a Karaite beit din in Cairo (on f. 27r of Yevr.-Arab. I 328). Dated Av 5325 AM (1565 CE). Purchase and profit-sharing agreement related to a civet cat (קט זבאד) that Ṣedaqa b. Yosef aka al-Naqqāsh bought from the seller Yaʿīsh b. ʿAbd al-Karīm al-Kohen aka al-Mawardī. The total price for the civet cat is listed as 35 gold ashrafī, a substantial sum of money. It does not seem that the buyer will take possession of the animal in order to care for it because one clause states that it will be left with Yaʿīsh, who agrees to provide it "food and drink" and to pay Ṣedaqa monthly installments of 28 medin, a sum "that corresponds to the profit that emerges from the civet cat" (ודלך פי נציר אלרבח אלדי יטלע פי אלקט אלזבאד). This debt is upon Yaʿīsh "whether [the civet cat] dies or lives" (ואן מאת ואן עאש עלי דמת יעיש). Lastly, the agreement states that the rest of the monthly profit "exceeding or less than" 28 medin will go to Yaʿīsh, the animal's caretaker. MCD.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בהספרייה הלאומית רוסיה.
